judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are very close in substance and quality across all four expectations; report 2 is marginally more precise on remediation specifics (e.g., recommending omission over toggling, and explicitly naming the cross-environment privilege-boundary risk), but neither explicitly calls for running/confirming the first migration, so they tie overall.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` — `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... Must be `"false"`. ... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:3` — `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... Must be `"false"`. ... `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:4` — `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`... Should be removed or set to `""`. ... the sink must point to a production-specific destination... either blank the sink or fix the code to gate on the flag. ... Needs a distinct, prod-specific secret ARN.

report_2.evidence: `infra/prod/app.auto.tfvars:2` `ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"`... it should be omitted from the prod tfvars entirely. ... `AUTH_RELAXED = "true"`... Must be removed (omitted, not set to "false") before apply. ... `PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"`... Must be omitted entirely from prod... Both keys should be omitted from prod, not toggled. ... It should not be recreated in prod; if billing genuinely needs a secret, it needs its own prod-scoped ARN, not this copy.

report_1.evidence: the API base URL is compiled into the static JS as a literal string, not read at runtime. ... it comes from `frontend/.env.staging:1`, because the one and only build in the pipeline always runs in staging mode. Since the identical image digest is deployed to prod, the production bundle will call `https://staging-api.example.com` for every API request — this value is frozen into the static JS at build time

report_2.evidence: VITE_-prefixed variables are inlined into the built bundle at build time." ... So the frontend bundle shipped to production would point at `https://staging-api.example.com`, because there is no separate production build step and no `.env.production` to source a correct value from even if one were added.

report_1.evidence: `infra/prod/main.tf:3` has the **exact same string**, `oidc_sub_condition = "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:staging"`, uncorrected from the copy... The correct fix is changing `infra/prod/main.tf:3` to `"repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production"` before applying.

report_2.evidence: the trust condition as written matches the *staging* job's token, meaning if this were applied unmodified, a `deploy-staging` job's OIDC token could potentially assume the *production* deploy role instead — a cross-environment privilege boundary violation
